name: Cerro de San Pedro Trail
name_original: Cerro de San Pedro — ida y vuelta por el Cerro de la Prestancia
description: Hike the Cerro de San Pedro — round trip via Cerro de la Prestancia, a moderate 2.7-mile route with 1134 feet of elevation gain and panorami
region_intro: Hiking around Soto Del Real offers access to diverse landscapes within the Sierra de Guadarrama, including parts of the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park and the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. The region is characterized by its gneiss and granite geology, featuring distinctive rock formations like those found in La Pedriza. Water resources are abundant, with the Manzanares River and several streams flowing into the Santillana reservoir, while vegetation ranges from dehesa pastureland at lower altitudes to Pyrenean oak and juniper forests higher up. Best hiking trails around Soto Del Real The most popular hiking route is Cerro de San Pedro Trail, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) moderate trail that takes 1 hour 45 minutes to complete, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Collado de la Dehesilla — circular desde Soto del Real, a difficult 6.5 miles (10.5 km) path featuring a substantial elevation change. Local hikers also love the La Raja de La Pedriza y Embalse de los Palancares — circular desde Soto del Real, an 8.2 miles (13.3 km) trail leading through the unique granite rock formations of La Pedriza, often completed in about 3 hours 39 minutes. Hiking around Soto Del Real is defined by the dramatic Sierra de Guadarrama mountains, the unique granite formations of La Pedriza, and diverse vegetation zones. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Soto Del Real are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 160 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Soto Del Real's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Soto del Real?
A: Soto del Real offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 40 distinct trails available. These routes cater to all levels, from easy strolls to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What makes the hiking landscape in Soto del Real unique?
A: The region's unique character comes from its location within the Sierra de Guadarrama, featuring distinctive gneiss and granite geology, especially notable in areas like La Pedriza with its iconic rock formations. You'll also find diverse vegetation zones, from dehesa pastureland to Pyrenean oak and juniper forests, all within the protected environments of the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park and Sierra de Guadarrama National Park.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Soto del Real?
A: Yes, Soto del Real has 5 easy trails perfect for beginners or families. While not explicitly listed as 'easy' in the guide, shorter moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383055982/cerro-de-san-pedro-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de San Pedro Trail&lt;/a&gt; (4.4 km) offer manageable distances and rewarding views without extreme difficulty.

Q: Can I find challenging long-distance hikes in Soto del Real?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, there are 12 difficult routes. Trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1229534121/collado-de-la-dehesilla-circular-desde-soto-del-real" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de la Dehesilla — circular desde Soto del Real&lt;/a&gt; (10.5 km) or the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383068537/dehesas-de-soto-el-hueco-de-san-blas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesas de Soto - El Hueco de San Blas Trail&lt;/a&gt; (18.4 km) feature substantial elevation changes and require good physical condition.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Soto del Real?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Soto del Real are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383063930/la-raja-de-la-pedriza-y-embalse-de-los-palancares-circular-desde-soto-del-real" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Raja de La Pedriza y Embalse de los Palancares — circular desde Soto del Real&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through unique granite formations and past the Palancares Reservoir.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls in the Soto del Real area?
A: While Soto del Real itself is rich in water resources, the wider region of Sierra de Guadarrama offers several beautiful waterfalls. You can explore nearby highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/851377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Purgatory Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2685066" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Arroyo de la Vejiga&lt;/a&gt;, which are accessible via trails in the surrounding areas.

Q: What kind of viewpoints can I expect to find on hikes in Soto del Real?
A: Hiking in Soto del Real offers numerous panoramic viewpoints, especially from higher elevations. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383055982/cerro-de-san-pedro-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de San Pedro Trail&lt;/a&gt; provide expansive vistas of the surrounding Sierra de Guadarrama mountains and the Hueco de San Blas valley. The peak of La Najarra also offers impressive views for those undertaking more challenging ascents.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sights to see while hiking in Soto del Real?
A: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, you can encounter cultural attractions. The Hermitage of Our Lady of the Rosary offers picturesque views. The area also features historical elements like the Puente de los Once Ojos (Bridge of Eleven Arches) and a medieval Romanesque Bridge, adding a cultural dimension to your hike.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Soto del Real?
A: The komoot community highly rates hiking in Soto del Real, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 160 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the dramatic Sierra de Guadarrama mountains, the unique granite formations of La Pedriza, and the diverse vegetation zones that make each route distinct and rewarding.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Soto del Real?
A: Soto del Real offers hiking opportunities year-round, but spring and autumn are particularly pleasant due to milder temperatures and vibrant natural colors. In spring, you'll see diverse vegetation and possibly wild jasmine and orchids. Summer can be hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable. Winter hikes are possible, especially at lower altitudes, but higher trails may require appropriate gear for snow and ice.

Q: Are there specific areas to explore within Soto del Real that are particularly scenic?
A: Absolutely. The Hueco de San Blas, a significant mountain valley, offers diverse views and deep exploration. La Pedriza is another must-see, renowned for its unique granite rock formations. Both areas are integrated into various hiking routes, providing distinct and memorable scenic experiences.
tips: The ascent can be mastered with individual tips, but with appropriate shoes
Great view from 1423 meters. A summit book is waiting at the top.

faqs.question: What is the typical duration for this hike?
faqs.answer: This round trip typically takes around 1 hour and 45 minutes to complete, allowing you to enjoy the scenery at a comfortable pace.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, despite some elevation gain, the trail is rated as 'Easy (1/15)' and is accessible for a wide range of hikers. The ascent to the summit is rapid but straightforward.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features varied terrain, including mountain trails, dirt roads, and a small section alongside an asphalt road. You'll also encounter stone walls along the path.

faqs.question: What can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The trail offers fantastic views, especially from the Cerro de San Pedro Summit. From there, you can see the reservoir of Santillana, La Pedriza, the Sierra de la Cabrera, and even the city of Madrid. The return journey provides beautiful views of the Sierra de Guadarrama foothills.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details aren't provided, the trail is located near Soto del Real in the Sierra de Guadarrama. Hikers typically find parking options in or near the starting point of such popular routes in the area.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally considered dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is under control, especially when encountering other hikers or wildlife, and check for any local leash regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Cerro de San Pedro — round trip via Cerro de la Prestancia trail.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer mornings are also great to avoid the midday heat.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or pubs near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near Soto del Real, Spain, which offers various amenities including cafes, pubs, and accommodation options where you can refresh before or after your hike.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, a significant portion of this route, approximately 35%, follows the official trail known as the Cordel de las Merinas.

faqs.question: What makes the Cerro de San Pedro unique?
faqs.answer: The Cerro de San Pedro is notable as an 'inselberg,' meaning it stands isolated from other mountains. This unique geological formation provides unobstructed and panoramic vistas from its peak, making the Ascent to Cerro de San Pedro particularly rewarding.

waypoints.name: Ascent to Cerro de San Pedro
